source.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

claim_chain: LCD self-association is a biological phenomenon regulating cellular signaling
Chemical properties governing LCD self-association must be established with mechanistic detail
Synthetic protein chemistry applied to understand how LCDs exploit polypeptide backbone principles
Inversion of Cα stereochemistry produces significant position-dependent effects
Data strongly support existence of structural elements essential for proper self-association
ChIM and synthetic mutagenesis methods will unearth additional LCD sequence features

typical_pattern: State biological phenomenon and knowledge gap
Interpret findings in context of existing models
Discuss broader implications and future directions

likely_priorities: Mechanistic detail and structural evidence for LCD interactions
Generalizability of enantioselectivity findings to other LCDs
Compatibility with existing multivalency and compositional models
Positional resolution advantages over traditional biochemical approaches
likely_attacks: Limited scope: extent to which enantioselectivity applies to other LCD:LCD interactions unclear
Generalizability concerns about findings from EMD and NEFL to broader LCD families
Need for additional validation of structural elements beyond stereochemistry
Mechanistic details of how geometric constraints translate to functional specificity
reviewer_preference_profile.inference_basis: The paper explicitly states 'The extent to which enantioselectivity will be applicable to other LCD:LCD interactions will be a subject of future study' and emphasizes that their mechanism 'is not mutually exclusive with the aforementioned models.' The authors also note that their approach 'remains challenging when limited to traditional biochemical approaches,' suggesting awareness that reviewers might question methodological necessity. The paper defends its structural model by stating data 'strongly support' it while acknowledging other models exist.

examples.quoted_phrase: our data strongly support the existence of structural elements that are essential for proper self-association
The extent to which enantioselectivity will be applicable to other LCD:LCD interactions will be a subject of future study
this mechanism is not mutually exclusive with the aforementioned models
we have shown that incorporation of an all-D C-terminal fragment results in EMD self-association similar to that of the WT LCD
fundamental questions remain regarding how polypeptide chemical and structural features beyond side chain identity contribute to LCD–LCD interactions

phrase_evidence.phrase: LCD self-association activity is a biological phenomenon that regulates cellular signaling processes throughout the cell in both physiological and disease states
To achieve an understanding of LCD biochemistry comparable to that of globular proteins
the chemical properties that govern LCD self-association and interaction specificity must be established with equivalent mechanistic detail and rigor
we apply synthetic protein chemistry to understand how LCDs exploit fundamental principles of the polypeptide backbone
inversion of Cα stereochemistry produces significant position-dependent effects on LCD self-association
our data strongly support the existence of structural elements that are essential for proper self-association
Among our most striking experimental findings that highlight this model
we have shown that incorporation of an all-D C-terminal fragment results in EMD self-association similar to that of the WT LCD
we and others have previously proposed labile cross-β interactions or β-bridging as a common interaction mechanism
Importantly, we emphasize that this mechanism is not mutually exclusive with the aforementioned models
The extent to which enantioselectivity will be applicable to other LCD:LCD interactions will be a subject of future study
Our data add to and are consistent with observations of self-association hotspots within LCDs of low sequence complexity
Our study demonstrates how synthetic protein chemistry can be leveraged to probe the chemical basis of LCD–LCD interactions with a high degree of positional resolution
which remains challenging when limited to traditional biochemical approaches
We envision that further application of ChIM and synthetic mutagenesis methods will unearth additional LCD sequence features
providing insight into diseases driven by aberrant LCD self-association
fundamental questions remain regarding how polypeptide chemical and structural features beyond side chain identity contribute to LCD–LCD interactions
it remains unclear to what extent similar geometric constraints apply to LCD self-association
By introducing targeted L-to-D amino acid inversions, ChIM identifies Cα stereocenters under geometric constraint without modification of side-chain functionalities
chiral inversion produces strongly position-dependent effects upon LCD self-association
Our study describes essential structural features that enable LCD self-association and chemical strategies to interrogate LCD biochemistry
AlphaFold-predicted structure of the full-length EMD protein and accompanying domain map
The synthetic scheme used to assemble the EMD ChIM library
ChIM scanning identifies enantioselective interaction hotspots in the self-associating region of EMD
ChIM scanning identifies self-association hotspots within the NEFL head domain

verification_status.notes: All extracted phrases are literal substrings from the input text. No external references, invented numbers, or author names were included. Claim strength profile reflects the paper's balanced approach of making strong mechanistic claims while acknowledging limitations and future work needed.

_phrase_violations.phrase: Our data add to and are consistent with observations of self-association hotspots within LCDs of low sequence complexity
